2606.13236.title: Decoding Insect Song: A Multitask Semisupervised Orthoptera Bioacoustic Classifier
2606.13236.paper_summary: Passive acoustic monitoring holds great promise for ecological inference, yet existing automated tools are typically narrowly trained and non-transferable. We address these limitations with PULSE, a semi-supervised, multi-task framework for Orthoptera bioacoustics, combining weakly-supervised species classification, self-supervised learning on unlabelled field audio, and knowledge distillation from a general-purpose bioacoustic model. Our domain-adapted specialist model outperforms a state-of-the-art general model across all metrics (macro F1: 0.21 vs. 0.07; AUC: 0.74 vs. 0.45; AP: 0.32 vs. 0.19), with active learning further raising F1 to 0.34 and AUC to 0.84. Beyond classification, the learned embeddings encode ecologically meaningful structure, exposed through an interactive visualisation tool for ecological discovery.
2606.13236.paper_summary_zh: 被動聲學監測對於生態推斷具有很大的潛力，然而現有的自動化工具通常訓練範圍狹窄且不可轉移。<lb/>我們通過PULSE來解決這些限制，這是一個半監督的多任務框架，專注於直翅目生物聲學，結合了弱監督的物種分類、對未標記野外音頻的自我監督學習，以及來自通用生物聲學模型的知識蒸餾。<lb/>我們的領域適應專家模型在所有指標上均優於最先進的通用模型（宏觀F1：0.21對0.07；AUC：0.74對0.45；AP：0.32對0.19），主動學習進一步將F1提升至0.34，AUC提升至0.84。<lb/>除了分類，學習到的嵌入編碼了生態上有意義的結構，通過一個互動可視化工具揭示，促進生態發現。
2606.13236.author: Olga Isupova et.al.
2606.13236.authors: Olga Isupova, Danil Kuzin, Ella Browning, Tom Mills, Steven Reece
